gold_requirements: Confirm duplicate charge will be investigated
incoming_email: Hi, I just looked at my card statement and CloudSync charged me TWICE this month for my Pro plan - once on the 1st and again on the 3rd. Invoice numbers are INV-88213 and INV-88240. I need this fixed and my money back. This is really frustrating, I use this card for other business expenses too. - Priya Nair, Bramble Design Co.
ground_truth_reply:
Hi Priya,

I'm sorry for the frustration this has caused, especially since it affects your business card. I looked into invoices INV-88213 and INV-88240 and I can confirm you were billed twice for the same billing cycle due to a processing error on our end.

I've submitted a refund for the duplicate charge (INV-88240) to our billing team. You should see the credit back on your card within 5-7 business days, depending on your bank's processing time.

I've also flagged your account to prevent this from happening again next cycle. You'll receive an email confirmation once the refund is processed.

Please let me know if you don't see the credit by next week or if there's anything else I can help with.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

incoming_email:
THIS IS UNACCEPTABLE. Our entire conveyancing team has been unable to sync case files since yesterday afternoon. The desktop app just sits at "Syncing 214 items" and never finishes. We are on Windows 11, CloudSync Desktop v4.12.1. We have court deadlines TOMORROW and cannot access updated documents. I need someone to call me immediately. This is costing us real money and putting client matters at risk.

David Whitfield
Senior Partner, Whitfield & Combe Solicitors
ground_truth_reply:
Hi David,

I understand the urgency here, especially with court deadlines tomorrow, and I'm sorry for the disruption this is causing your team. I've raised this as a high-priority issue with our engineering team under ticket ENG-51402 so it gets immediate attention.

To help them diagnose the stuck sync quickly, could you send over the sync log from CloudSync Desktop (Settings > Help > Export Logs) along with the affected folder names? This will let us pinpoint whether it's a file conflict or a connection issue.

In the meantime, you can access the most recent saved versions of your case files directly through cloudsync.com in your browser, which does not depend on the desktop sync process.

I know phone support isn't standard for your plan, but given the urgency I'm escalating this internally and will follow up as soon as I hear back from engineering, and no later than end of day.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

incoming_email: hey! quick q - how do i share a folder with my students so they can only view the files, not edit or delete anything? i teach high school history and don't want anyone accidentally deleting stuff lol. thanks!
ground_truth_reply:
Hi there,

Great question - this is easy to set up. When you share a folder, click the "Share" button, add your students' emails, and set their permission level to "Viewer" instead of "Editor." Viewers can open and download files but can't edit, move, or delete anything.

If you want to allow comments without full editing, there's also a "Commenter" role that sits in between.

You can find a full walkthrough with screenshots here: https://docs.cloudsync.com/sharing/permission-levels

Let me know if you run into any trouble setting it up!

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

gold_requirements: Explain how to request the report
Offer to loop in account manager
incoming_email:
Good afternoon,

As part of our vendor risk assessment process, our procurement team requires confirmation of CloudSync's current SOC 2 Type II compliance status, along with a copy of the most recent audit report if available. We also have a 40-question security questionnaire that needs to be completed within 10 business days per our internal vendor onboarding policy.

Could you direct me to the appropriate contact or process for this?

Regards,
Monica Alvarez
Vendor Risk Analyst, Sterling Capital Partners
ground_truth_reply:
Hi Monica,

Thank you for reaching out, and happy to help with your vendor risk assessment. CloudSync currently maintains SOC 2 Type II compliance, and our audit report is available to enterprise customers under a mutual NDA.

I'll loop in your dedicated account manager, who can arrange the NDA and share the report directly, since that process goes through our security team rather than general support.

For the security questionnaire, we typically provide a completed template covering common frameworks (SOC 2, GDPR, data residency, encryption practices) to speed things up on your end - your account manager can send that over as well.

Given your 10-business-day timeline, I'd recommend connecting with them as soon as possible; I've flagged this as high priority so they reach out today.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

must_not_include: share account recovery codes over email
incoming_email: i lost my phone yesterday and cant get into my cloudsync account because it wants a code from my authenticator app which was ON that phone. i have a deadline in 2 hours for a client video that's sitting in my account. please just turn off 2fa for me so i can log in!!
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

I'm sorry about your phone, and I understand the pressure with your deadline. For security reasons, I can't disable two-factor authentication without verifying it's really you first - this protects your account from anyone else trying the same request.

To verify your identity, could you reply with:
1. The email address on the account
2. The last 4 digits of the payment method on file
3. A recent invoice number or file name from your account

Once verified, I can help you regain access, typically within 30 minutes during business hours. If you have backup codes saved from when you set up MFA, those would let you log in immediately without waiting.

I know the clock is ticking - reply with the details above and I'll prioritize this right away.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

gold_requirements: Explain how to add VAT number
incoming_email: Hello, our finance department noticed that our last three invoices (INV-77102, INV-77340, INV-77581) do not show our VAT number (DE291837465), so we cannot properly file for reverse charge. Could you correct these invoices and confirm the VAT number is stored on our account going forward? Thank you, Lukas Herrmann, Finance, Nordkette Retail GmbH
ground_truth_reply:
Hi Lukas,

Thanks for flagging this. I can see your account doesn't currently have a VAT number on file, which is why it wasn't reflected on the invoices.

You can add your VAT number under Billing > Company Details in your admin dashboard - once saved, it will automatically appear on all future invoices. For the three existing invoices (INV-77102, INV-77340, INV-77581), I've submitted a request to our billing team to reissue corrected copies with your VAT number (DE291837465) included; these should arrive by email within 2-3 business days.

You can review all invoices anytime at https://cloudsync.com/billing/invoices.

Please let us know if you don't see the corrected invoices by the end of the week.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

incoming_email: Our quarterly-report-final.docx file is showing as 0 KB and won't open in Word anymore. It worked fine yesterday. This is genuinely disappointing given we pay for the Pro plan expecting reliability. Is there any way to recover this? We have a board meeting in 2 days.
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

I'm sorry to hear about quarterly-report-final.docx - I understand how stressful this is with your board meeting coming up.

Before anything else, please check the file's version history: right-click the file, select "Version History," and look for a version from before it showed as 0 KB. In most cases, this lets you restore a working copy in seconds, and I'd recommend trying this first at https://docs.cloudsync.com/files/version-history.

If no earlier version is available or the file is corrupted across all versions, please let me know right away and I'll open an engineering ticket (I'll share the ticket number here) so our team can investigate whether the file can be recovered from backup. I can't guarantee full recovery in that case, but we'll do everything possible.

Let me know what you find in version history and we'll go from there.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

must_not_include: change the email on their behalf without verification
incoming_email: Good morning, I recently switched from my personal Gmail to my organization's email address and would like to update my CloudSync account accordingly. Could you please guide me through the process? Thank you for your time. Kind regards, Ananya Rao, Prerna Foundation
ground_truth_reply:
Hi Ananya,

Good morning, and thanks for reaching out. You can update your email address yourself under Account Settings > Profile > Email Address. After entering the new address, we'll send a verification link to it - once you click that link, the change takes effect immediately and you'll use the new email to log in going forward.

A quick note: your old email will stop working for login as soon as the new one is verified, so make sure you have access to it before starting the switch.

Full steps with screenshots are here: https://docs.cloudsync.com/account/change-email

Let me know if you run into any issues.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

incoming_email:
The CloudSync desktop app crashes within 10 seconds of opening on every machine in our design department (12 machines, all macOS Sonoma 14.5). This started right after the app auto-updated to version 5.2.0 last night. We cannot access any of our CAD files right now. Fix this today.

Regional IT Manager - Torque Fabrication Inc.
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

Thank you for flagging this immediately - a crash affecting 12 machines right after an update is serious, and I'm sorry for the disruption to your design department.

I've opened an urgent engineering ticket, ENG-51488, specifically for the v5.2.0 crash on macOS Sonoma 14.5. To help the team pinpoint the cause, could someone send the crash log from one affected machine? You can find it at ~/Library/Logs/CloudSync/crash-report-latest.log.

As an immediate workaround, your team can access all files through the browser at cloudsync.com without needing the desktop app, so your CAD files aren't inaccessible in the meantime - though I understand that's not ideal for your workflow.

I'll follow up as soon as I have an update from engineering, and will treat this as top priority given the scope of impact.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

incoming_email: Love CloudSync so far! One small request though - any chance of a dark mode for the desktop app? I work late a lot and the bright white interface is rough on the eyes at midnight :) Thanks!
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

Thanks so much for the kind words, and for the suggestion - you're definitely not the first night owl to ask for this! I've logged your request for dark mode with our product team under our feature request tracker (reference FR-2291).

I can't promise a timeline since I don't want to overpromise on something still under consideration, but customer feedback like yours genuinely helps shape what we prioritize next.

In the meantime, some users find that adjusting their OS-level display settings (like macOS Night Shift or Windows Night Light) helps reduce eye strain when using CloudSync late at night.

Thanks again for taking the time to share this!

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

gold_requirements: Escalate to engineering with ticket ID
incoming_email:
We configured SCIM provisioning between Okta and CloudSync last week. New users are being created correctly, but when we deactivate a user in Okta, their CloudSync account remains active instead of being deprovisioned. This is a security gap for us since offboarded employees can still access company files. Our Okta SCIM logs show 200 OK responses for the deactivation calls, so the issue appears to be on CloudSync's side.

Jordan Blake, IT Security, Meridian Systems
ground_truth_reply:
Hi Jordan,

Thank you for the detailed report - this is an important security gap and I want to get it resolved quickly. Since Okta is returning 200 OK on the deactivation calls but the CloudSync account isn't being deprovisioned, this does point to a processing issue on our end rather than your Okta configuration.

I've opened engineering ticket ENG-51503 with high priority. To speed up the investigation, could you share one or two example usernames/emails where deactivation didn't propagate, along with the approximate timestamps of the Okta deactivation calls?

As an immediate mitigation, your admins can manually deactivate users under Admin Dashboard > Users while engineering investigates the SCIM sync issue - I know that's an extra step, but it closes the security gap in the interim.

Our SCIM documentation is here for reference: https://docs.cloudsync.com/enterprise/scim-provisioning

I'll update you as soon as I hear back from engineering.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

incoming_email: sorry if this is a dumb question but i accidentally deleted a whole folder of my thesis notes yesterday??? is there any way to get it back i am panicking a little
ground_truth_reply:
Hi, no worries at all - this happens more often than you'd think, and it's fixable!

Deleted files and folders go to your Trash, where they stay recoverable for 30 days before being permanently removed. To restore your thesis notes folder: go to Trash in the left sidebar, find the folder, right-click it, and select "Restore." It will go right back to its original location.

Here's a quick guide with screenshots: https://docs.cloudsync.com/files/restore-from-trash

Since it was only deleted yesterday, you should have no trouble getting it back. Let me know if you don't see it in the Trash and I'll dig further.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

gold_requirements: Ask for request IDs or timestamps
incoming_email:
URGENT - our clinic staff across 6 locations cannot load patient intake documents through CloudSync web app right now. Pages are timing out or returning 502 errors. This has been going on for 25 minutes. We have patients waiting. Request ID from one attempt: req_9f83ab21. Please advise immediately, this is a patient care issue.

Dr. Samuel Okoye, Clinical Operations Director, Riverside Health Group
ground_truth_reply:
Dr. Okoye,

I understand the seriousness of this given the impact on patient care, and I'm sorry for the disruption. I've escalated this immediately as critical priority under ticket ENG-51522, referencing request ID req_9f83ab21.

Could you also confirm the approximate time the issue started and whether this affects all 6 locations or specific ones? That will help engineering narrow down the cause faster.

You can monitor real-time updates on our system status page at https://status.cloudsync.com, which we update as soon as we have more information.

I've also notified my manager given the severity, and someone from our team will follow up directly with you as this progresses. I know every minute matters here.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

Hi,

Thanks for the detailed report - that makes this easier to troubleshoot. A 401 with "invalid_token" right after generating a fresh token usually means one of two things: the token wasn't included with the "Bearer " prefix in the Authorization header, or it's being sent in the wrong header entirely (some clients default to a query param).

Could you double check your header looks like:
Authorization: Bearer <your_token>

If that's already correct, the token may have been scoped without file:write permissions - you can check scopes under Developer Settings > API Tokens.

On rate limits: bulk uploads are capped at 100 requests per minute per token on the Pro plan, and you'll get a 429 response with a Retry-After header if you exceed it.

Full API reference is here: https://docs.cloudsync.com/api/authentication

Let me know if the header fix doesn't resolve it and I'll dig into request_id req_44210x directly.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

incoming_email: Well, I guess it's time to cancel. We've been "evaluating our options" for a while now (your words, not mine, from every marketing email) and honestly a competitor just does more for less. Please process the cancellation for our Business plan, subscription ID SUB-30215. Thanks for nothing new in the last year.
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

Thanks for letting us know, and I'm sorry CloudSync hasn't kept up with what you need - that's honest feedback and I'll pass it along internally.

I can process the cancellation for subscription SUB-30215. Before I do, one quick note: I'd recommend exporting any files or data you want to keep, since access ends at the close of your current billing period. You can export everything under Account Settings > Export Data, or I'm happy to confirm your renewal date first if that's helpful.

If a lower-tier plan would ever cover your needs instead of a full cancellation, that's an option too, but I won't push it if you've already made up your mind.

Just reply "confirm" and I'll process the cancellation right away, effective at the end of your current billing cycle.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

gold_requirements: Ask about file size and connection
incoming_email: Every time I try to upload a video file over 2GB through the web browser it fails right at 99% with a generic "Upload failed" message. Tried Chrome and Firefox, same result both times. This is really frustrating because I'm on a deadline for a client delivery.
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

That's a frustrating spot to be in, especially failing right at 99% - I'm sorry for the trouble. Browser uploads over 2GB can be more prone to interruption since they depend on a stable connection for the entire upload window without any retry logic.

For large video files, I'd recommend using the CloudSync Desktop App instead - it uploads in chunks and can automatically resume if your connection drops, which usually resolves this exact issue: https://cloudsync.com/download

If the desktop app also fails on this file, please let me know the exact file size and your desktop app version, and I'll escalate to engineering to check for any upload limits or corruption on the file itself.

Hopefully the desktop app gets your client delivery back on track - let me know how it goes.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

incoming_email:
We rotated our SAML signing certificate in Azure AD yesterday as part of routine maintenance and updated the metadata in CloudSync's SSO settings accordingly. Now all users get "SAML assertion validation failed" when trying to log in via SSO. We've double-checked the metadata URL is correct and current. Can someone look into this from CloudSync's side? We have 3,000 users unable to log in.

Rachel Kim, Identity & Access Management, Halden Financial Group
ground_truth_reply:
Hi Rachel,

Thank you for the clear write-up - a certificate rotation causing assertion validation failures across 3,000 users is high impact, and I want to get this resolved quickly. I've opened engineering ticket ENG-51547 with high priority.

A common cause after certificate rotation is a caching delay on our side where the old certificate fingerprint is still being used to validate assertions, even though your new metadata is correctly published. Could you confirm the exact time you updated the metadata in CloudSync, and send over the SAML response from one failed login attempt (with sensitive fields redacted) if possible?

Our SSO/SAML documentation is here for reference: https://docs.cloudsync.com/enterprise/saml-configuration

I'll keep you updated as engineering investigates, and will treat this as a priority given the number of users affected.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

incoming_email: how much storage do i get on the free plan?
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

The Free plan includes 5 GB of storage. If you ever need more space, Pro plans start at 500 GB, but there's no pressure - the Free plan works great for lighter personal use.

You can check your current usage anytime under Account Settings > Storage.

Let me know if you have any other questions!

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

must_not_include: guarantee specific contract terms without legal review

Our legal counsel requires an executed Data Processing Agreement (DPA) with CloudSync before we can continue using it to store client documents subject to GDPR. Could you send the current DPA and, if available, a list of your sub-processors? We'd like to move quickly on this - our compliance review is due next Friday.

Elena Vasquez, Legal Counsel, Brightwell & Cole LLP
ground_truth_reply:
Hi Elena,

Thank you for reaching out. CloudSync does provide a standard Data Processing Agreement covering GDPR requirements, along with a current sub-processors list.

Since executing a DPA involves our legal team rather than general support, I'll connect you with them directly to get this moving given your Friday deadline. They'll send over the DPA template and sub-processor list for your counsel's review, and can also address any redline requests from there.

In the meantime, our general privacy and compliance practices are documented at https://cloudsync.com/legal/privacy if that's useful background for your review.

I've flagged this as time-sensitive so our legal team reaches out today.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

must_not_include: share details about the failed login attempts' origin without checking
incoming_email: my account got locked after i typed my password wrong a bunch of times trying to remember it, how long do i have to wait?? i need to get a file for a meeting in 20 min
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

I understand the time pressure - accounts lock automatically after multiple failed attempts as a security measure, and normally that lasts 30 minutes before you can try again.

To unlock it sooner, I can verify your identity and remove the lock manually. Could you reply with:
1. The email address on the account
2. The last 4 digits of the payment method on file (if Pro is on a card) or your billing zip code

Once I verify these, I can unlock your account right away rather than making you wait out the full 30 minutes.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

gold_requirements: Offer to connect with sales/account manager for enterprise pricing
must_not_include: quote exact enterprise pricing without sales involvement
incoming_email: We're currently on the Business plan with 85 seats and want to move to Enterprise for the SSO and audit log features. How does billing work for a mid-cycle upgrade - do we get charged the full new rate right away or is it prorated? Also who do I talk to about custom seat pricing at our volume?
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

Great to hear you're looking at Enterprise - SSO and audit logs are popular reasons for that move. For a mid-cycle upgrade, we prorate the difference: you'd be charged only for the remaining days in your current billing cycle at the new Enterprise rate, and then billed the full Enterprise rate starting next cycle.

Since Enterprise pricing at 85 seats often involves custom volume discounts, that's handled by our sales/account management team rather than general billing - I'll connect you with your account manager so they can put together pricing tailored to your seat count.

You can see a general feature comparison in the meantime at https://cloudsync.com/pricing.

Let me know if you'd like me to loop in your account manager directly.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

gold_requirements: Acknowledge lack of native Linux support
must_not_include: claim Linux support exists when it doesn't
incoming_email: Kind of surprised there's no native Linux desktop client in 2026. I run Ubuntu 24.04 for all my dev work and having to use the browser for everything is a real step down from the Windows/Mac experience my coworkers get. Any plans here?
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

That's fair feedback, and I understand the gap - we currently don't have a native Linux desktop client, so Ubuntu users are limited to the web app, which I know isn't feature-equivalent to the native apps.

I've logged your request for Linux support under FR-1187, which already has interest from other customers, so it's good to have another voice adding weight to it.

I can't promise a release date since it's still under evaluation, but in the meantime, the web app at cloudsync.com does support most core features including file sync via manual upload/download, comments, and version history.

Thanks for taking the time to share this - feedback like yours does influence what we prioritize.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

incoming_email: Hi there, I'd like to set up two-factor authentication on my account for extra security. Could you walk me through the steps? Thanks in advance!
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

Great idea - happy to walk you through it. Go to Account Settings > Security > Two-Factor Authentication and click "Enable." You'll scan a QR code with an authenticator app like Google Authenticator or Authy, then enter the 6-digit code it generates to confirm setup.

Important: right after enabling it, you'll be shown a set of backup codes - save these somewhere safe (like a password manager), since they're the only way back into your account if you ever lose access to your authenticator app.

Full steps with screenshots: https://docs.cloudsync.com/security/enable-mfa

Let me know if you hit any snags during setup.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

gold_requirements: Avoid assuming user error without checking
must_not_include: assume it's user error without investigating
incoming_email: This doesn't make sense - I set my intern's account to "Viewer" on our /Marketing/2026-Campaigns folder but he was still able to edit and even delete a file inside it yesterday. I definitely set it to Viewer, I checked twice. Is this a bug or am I missing something?
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

That's understandably confusing, especially after double-checking the setting. Viewer permissions should strictly prevent editing or deleting, so this does sound like it could be a bug rather than a configuration issue on your end.

A couple of things that could explain it: permissions set at a subfolder level can sometimes override the parent folder's setting, or the intern may have inherited Editor access from a different shared link. Could you confirm the exact file name that was deleted and whether /Marketing/2026-Campaigns has any subfolders with different permission settings?

In the meantime, I'm opening ticket ENG-51560 so our engineering team can check whether Viewer permissions are being correctly enforced. I'll keep you posted once I hear back.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

gold_requirements: Mention data is not deleted immediately
must_not_include: claim data is deleted immediately after trial ends
incoming_email: why cant i access my files anymore? it just says my trial ended. i didnt even realize it was time limited
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

Good question - CloudSync's free trial runs for 14 days, and once it ends, the account switches to a limited state where you can log in but can't access files until you subscribe to a paid plan.

The good news: your files aren't deleted, they're just paused. To get everything back, simply choose a plan under Account Settings > Billing > Choose a Plan, and your files will be immediately accessible again.

You can compare plans here: https://cloudsync.com/pricing

Let me know if you have questions about which plan fits your needs.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

gold_requirements: Explain how to access/export audit logs
incoming_email: For an internal security review, I need to export the full audit log for our organization covering the last 90 days, including login events, file deletions, and permission changes. Where can I download this, and how far back does CloudSync retain audit data for Enterprise accounts?
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

Happy to help with your security review. You can export audit logs under Admin Dashboard > Audit Logs > Export, where you can filter by date range and event type (logins, file deletions, permission changes, etc.) before downloading as a CSV.

For Enterprise accounts, audit log data is retained for 12 months, so your 90-day export should be fully available.

Full documentation on filtering and export options is here: https://docs.cloudsync.com/enterprise/audit-logs

Let me know if you need help interpreting any specific event types in the export.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

gold_requirements: Escalate if setting truly not saving
incoming_email: I am getting like 5 emails every time ANYONE comments on ANY file in my shared folder, even ones I'm not tagged in. I already turned off "all activity" notifications in settings and turned on only "@mentions" but it's still flooding my inbox. Please make it stop.
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

That sounds genuinely annoying, especially after you already changed the setting - it shouldn't still be sending you all-activity notifications once "@mentions only" is selected.

To confirm this is a bug rather than a setting that didn't save properly, could you check Account Settings > Notifications one more time and let me know if it currently shows "@mentions only" as selected? If it does, and you're still getting flooded, I'll open an engineering ticket to investigate why the preference isn't being respected.

As a temporary fix while we look into it, you can mute notifications for that specific shared folder by right-clicking it and selecting "Mute Notifications," which should stop the emails immediately.

Let me know what you find and I'll take it from there.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

must_not_include: invent a bulk-invite feature that doesn't exist if not confirmed
incoming_email: We just moved 15 new consultants onto our team. What's the fastest way to invite all of them to our CloudSync Business account, and will this affect our seat count/billing right away?
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

Congrats on the team growth! You can invite multiple people at once under Admin Dashboard > Team > Invite Members - just paste in all 15 email addresses separated by commas and CloudSync will send invitations to each.

On billing: adding seats does update your seat count immediately, and you'll be charged a prorated amount for the remaining days in your current billing cycle for each new seat, with the full rate applying from your next billing date.

Full steps here: https://docs.cloudsync.com/team/invite-members

Let me know if any of the invites don't go through and I'll help troubleshoot.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

incoming_email: Does CloudSync have any kind of AI-powered search where I could type something like 'the contract with the termination clause about 60 days notice' and it would find the right file, instead of me having to remember the exact file name?
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

Great question. Right now, CloudSync's search works based on file names, folder paths, and text found directly within supported document types (like Word docs and PDFs) - so it can find files by keyword content, but it doesn't yet understand natural-language queries like "the contract about 60 days notice" the way an AI-powered semantic search would.

I've logged this as a feature request under FR-2305, since AI-assisted search has come up from a few other customers too. I can't promise if or when this would be released, but I wanted to be upfront rather than suggest it already works that way.

In the meantime, searching for specific terms like "termination" or "60 days notice" within the search bar should surface documents containing that exact text.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

must_not_include: blame customer for typos without checking
incoming_email: Search has been basically useless for the past 2 days. I search for file names I KNOW exist, word for word, and get zero results. Then 10 minutes later the same search sometimes works. This is really slowing my team down.
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

I'm sorry search has been unreliable - that inconsistency (working, then not, then working again) does point to an indexing delay issue on our backend rather than anything wrong with your search terms.

Could you share one specific file name that failed to appear, along with roughly when you searched for it? That will help our engineering team trace the indexing gap.

I've opened ticket ENG-51578 to investigate the delay pattern you're describing. In the meantime, browsing directly through folders rather than search should still reliably show all your files while we sort this out.

I'll follow up once I hear back from engineering.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

must_not_include: claim data can be recovered after deletion
incoming_email: I'd like my CloudSync account and all associated data permanently deleted. I no longer use the service and want to exercise my right to erasure under GDPR. Please confirm once done, and confirm I won't be charged again.
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

Thank you for reaching out, and I can help process this. Under our GDPR-compliant deletion process, your account and all associated data will be permanently removed within 30 days of your request. Please note this is irreversible - once complete, we won't be able to recover any files or account information.

Since you're on the Free plan, there are no charges to worry about, but I want to confirm either way: no further billing will occur regardless.

To proceed, please reply "confirm deletion" so I have it in writing, and I'll initiate the process right away. You'll receive a final confirmation email once deletion is complete.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

gold_requirements: Clarify how Google login linking works
must_not_include: claim to change linked account without user action
incoming_email: when i click "sign in with google" it logs me into a totally different cloudsync account than the one i normally use with my email and password. how do i fix this so google login goes to my main account?
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

This happens because "Sign in with Google" links to whichever CloudSync account matches the Google email address you're currently signed into on your browser - if that's different from your main account's email, it'll log you into the wrong one.

To fix it: log into your main CloudSync account using email and password, then go to Account Settings > Connected Accounts > Link Google Account, and connect it there. Once linked, "Sign in with Google" will always go to that account going forward.

If the other account isn't something you recognize at all, let me know and I can look into it further.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

gold_requirements: Mention time estimate for large exports
must_not_include: promise instant export for very large accounts
incoming_email: As part of our annual data backup policy, I need to export all files and folders from our CloudSync Business account (approximately 2.1 TB across ~40,000 files). What's the process for a full bulk export, and how long should we expect it to take?
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

Happy to help with your annual backup. You can start a full export under Admin Dashboard > Data Management > Export All Data, which packages everything into downloadable zip archives split by folder for easier handling.

For an account of your size (~2.1 TB, ~40,000 files), the packaging process typically takes several hours before the download links are ready - you'll get an email notification once it's complete, so there's no need to keep the page open and wait.

Full details here: https://docs.cloudsync.com/admin/data-export

Let me know if the export doesn't complete within 24 hours and I'll look into it.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

gold_requirements: Explain available discount programs if any
Direct to appropriate team if needed
must_not_include: invent a discount program that doesn't exist
incoming_email: Hi! I run a small nonprofit and was wondering if CloudSync offers any nonprofit or annual billing discounts? Our budget is pretty tight this year.
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

Thanks for reaching out, and for the work you're doing. CloudSync does offer a discount for switching from monthly to annual billing, which brings the effective monthly cost down - you can see the exact annual pricing under Billing > Change Plan and toggling to "Annual."

For nonprofit-specific pricing, that's something our sales team handles on a case-by-case basis, so I'd recommend reaching out to them directly at sales@cloudsync.com with a bit about your organization - I don't want to promise a nonprofit discount here since I can't confirm eligibility or terms myself.

Hope that helps, and let me know if you have other questions in the meantime!

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

must_not_include: guarantee old versions will be recovered
incoming_email: I need someone senior to look at this NOW. Our shared /Finance/Budgets folder has completely lost its version history - files that had 40+ revisions yesterday now show ONLY the current version. We rely on version history for our audit trail. If this data is gone, we have a serious compliance problem. I want a manager on this today.
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

I understand why this is alarming given your audit trail requirements, and I take this seriously. I've escalated this immediately to engineering under ticket ENG-51602 with high priority, and I've also looped in my manager given the compliance implications you've described.

Could you confirm the exact file names in /Finance/Budgets affected, and roughly when you last confirmed the version history was intact? That will help engineering narrow down whether this is a display issue or an actual data problem.

I want to be transparent: I can't guarantee the older versions will be recoverable until engineering completes their investigation, but this has been flagged as urgent and we're treating it accordingly.

My manager or I will follow up with you directly within the next few hours.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

incoming_email: Would be awesome if CloudSync could integrate with Google Calendar so file deadlines/reminders could show up there automatically! Loving the product overall btw :)
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

Thanks so much, glad you're enjoying CloudSync! A Google Calendar integration for deadlines is a nice idea, and I've logged it as a feature request under FR-2318 so our product team sees the interest.

Right now, our existing integrations cover Slack, Microsoft Teams, Zapier, and Google Workspace (for file access, not calendar syncing specifically) - you can see the full list at https://cloudsync.com/integrations.

I can't promise if or when calendar integration would launch, but I appreciate you taking the time to suggest it!

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

must_not_include: blame the customer's zip software without checking
incoming_email: Trying to download our whole /Projects/Q3-Launch folder as a zip and the download completes but the zip file won't open - says it's corrupted/damaged in both Windows and on my colleague's Mac. Tried 3 times, same result each time.
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

That's frustrating, especially after multiple attempts with the same result across two different operating systems - that consistency suggests the issue is with how the zip is being generated on our end rather than your local systems.

Could you confirm roughly how large the /Projects/Q3-Launch folder is (total size and number of files)? Very large folders can sometimes hit a packaging issue during zip creation.

I've opened ticket ENG-51619 to investigate. As a workaround in the meantime, downloading files individually or in smaller sub-folder batches should work fine while we look into the full-folder zip issue.

I'll follow up once I hear back from engineering.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

incoming_email: We're building an internal automation on top of the CloudSync API and noticed webhooks are only available at the account level, not per-folder. This means we get flooded with events for folders we don't care about. Any plans to support folder-scoped webhook subscriptions? This would significantly reduce our processing overhead.
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

Thanks for the detailed feedback - that's a reasonable limitation to flag, and I can see why account-wide webhooks create unnecessary overhead for your use case.

Currently, our webhook events (file.created, file.updated, file.deleted, permission.changed) are indeed scoped at the account level rather than per-folder, so there isn't a way to filter subscriptions by folder today: https://docs.cloudsync.com/api/webhooks

I've logged this as a feature request under FR-2330 for folder-scoped webhook subscriptions, and included your use case as context for our API team.

As a workaround in the meantime, you could filter events client-side by checking the folder_id field in each webhook payload before processing, which at least avoids unnecessary automation runs even though it doesn't reduce the event volume itself.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

gold_requirements: Avoid promising full merge if unsupported
must_not_include: claim automatic merging is possible if it isn't
incoming_email: I accidentally signed up twice with two different emails and now have files split across both accounts. Is there a way to merge them into one account so everything is in the same place?
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

Thanks for explaining - this is a common mix-up. I want to be upfront: CloudSync doesn't currently support fully merging two accounts into one, since they're treated as entirely separate storage systems.

What I can do is help you consolidate manually: you can share folders from one account to the other (using both email addresses) and then copy the files over, or export from one account and re-upload into the other. If one of the accounts is Free and the other is Pro, I can also help make sure you're not paying for two subscriptions - let me know both emails (or the one you'd like to keep as primary) and I'll check with our billing team.

Let me know how you'd like to proceed and I'll guide you through it.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync

gold_requirements: Explain reason for failure if known
Provide steps to update payment method
must_not_include: threaten immediate suspension without grace period
incoming_email: Getting emails saying our payment failed for subscription SUB-41207. Card should be fine, we use it for everything else. How much time do we have before you cut off our access? We can't have downtime right now.
ground_truth_reply:
Hi,

Thanks for reaching out quickly on this. I checked subscription SUB-41207 and the payment failure was due to the card on file showing as expired as of last month - that would explain why it's declining even though the card works elsewhere, if a new one was recently issued.

You have a 7-day grace period from the first failed attempt before access is paused, so there's no immediate risk of downtime as long as this is resolved within that window.

To update your payment method, go to Billing > Payment Methods > Update Card, and the next billing attempt will run automatically once the new card is saved.

Let me know once it's updated, or if you'd like me to manually retry the charge after you've made the change.

Best,
Alex
Support Specialist, CloudSync
